cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out if following these steps brings me back to the starting point. Let me break it down step by step. First, I start facing forward. The initial direction is important because all movements are relative to facing forward. Let me assume that "forward" is the positive y-direction, "right" is the positive x-direction, and "left" is the negative x-direction. "Backward" would then be the negative y-direction. Let me track my position and direction as I follow each instruction. I'll represent my position with coordinates (x, y), starting at (0, 0). My initial direction is forward, so I'm facing along the positive y-axis. 1. Always face forward. So my direction remains forward throughout. 2. Take 3 steps right. Since I'm facing forward (along the y-axis), right would be the positive x-direction. So I move from (0, 0) to (3, 0). 3. Take 1 step backward. Backward is the negative y-direction. So I move from (3, 0) to (3, -1). 4. Take 6 steps left. Left is the negative x-direction. So I move from (3, -1) to (3 - 6, -1) = (-3, -1). 5. Take 1 step forward.
